title: 'Which side were you on during Brexit? Every candidate in the French presidential election should answer this question'
subhead: The lessons from the UK's unprecedented vote to leave the European Union 10 years ago have been all too quickly forgotten, writes Le Monde columnist Alain Frachon.

title: France records 2,025 excess deaths from heatwave, but data remains incomplete
The dashed hopes of France's isolated environment minister
Washington marks America's 250th anniversary with sloppy and politicized celebrations
subhead: Taken over by Donald Trump, the celebrations are set to culminate on Saturday with Independence Day. The pavilions installed on the National Mall in Washington, intended to honor history and popular culture, have failed to draw crowds.

title: Why a French court ordered the historic retrial of a man convicted of four murders 30 years ago
subhead: Dany Leprince has long maintained his innocence regarding the murders of his brother, sister-in-law and two nieces at their home in 1994. Criminal retrials are extremely rare in France.

title: Timothy Snyder, historian: 'Trump's war against Iran shows that evil and folly can march hand in hand'
subhead: The professor at the University of Toronto analyses the memorandum of understanding signed between the US and Iran, specifically its consequences on the image of the US president, whom he describes as 'the  architect of the brutal Iranian regime's revival'.

title: Food takes center stage at open air guinguettes along the Loire River
subhead: Fish fritters and carrot-top gazpacho are now on the menus of these convivial waterside spots. This marks a notable step up in quality for these casual dining and imbibing venues, which give pride of place to local products.

Four graphs to visualize the unprecedented scale of France's June heatwave
subhead: France's most recent heatwave began on June 17 and reached unprecedented levels in terms of both peak temperatures and official alert status. It proved to be more intense than the infamous 2003 heatwave and could also surpass its duration.

title: Early heatwaves are devastating French agriculture and leaving farmers helpless
subhead: Scorched crops, decimated livestock and double-digit production drops: as farmers take stock of the effects of extreme heat, they fear the worst is yet to come.

title: Christopher Nolan's 'The Odyssey' sparks a Homeric debate in Greece
subhead: The casting of a Black actress as Helen of Troy has ignited fierce controversy in Greece. Some lament what they see as Hollywood's appropriation of Greek mythology, while others emphasize the universal nature of these ancient stories.
